headline: Highway Rest Areas Fail Audits at 65% of Locations
context: PLUS and LLM audits found 65% of highway rest and service areas failed cleanliness and facility standards in 2025.

cards.big: 65% of highway rest areas failed cleanliness audits in 2025
cards.sub: Toilet conditions and food hygiene most common failure categories

cards.big: Random audits of 180 rest areas found 42% had unacceptable toilet facilities
cards.sub: Public complaints reached 28,000 through PLUS hotline in 2025

cards.big: Food safety violations recorded at 35% of rest area food outlets
cards.sub: Health Ministry issued 480 closure orders to highway food operators

cards.big: PLUS invested RM85 million upgrading 45 rest areas but 135 remain substandard
cards.sub: Concession agreement requires only minimum maintenance not quality improvement

cards.big: Rest area conditions test whether highway concessions include service quality obligations
65% of highway rest areas fail audits. Maintenance contracts without performance penalties produce decay.
